Who I Am

Ambitious.
Analytical.
Entrepreneurial.

My name is Lars van Lelyveld — a 24-year-old master's student and young professional based in Amsterdam. Originally from Friesland, I moved to Amsterdam to build the career and life I envisioned for myself.

I am deeply interested in the intersection of finance, real estate, sustainability, and strategic decision-making. What fascinates me most is understanding how organizations allocate capital, evaluate investment opportunities, and create long-term value in complex environments.

Integrity
Ambition

Reliability
Entrepreneurship
Sustainability
Continuous Learning
Personal Development

At a Glance

Building expertise at the intersection of finance, real estate & sustainability.

Throughout my studies and professional career I have consistently challenged myself — working in banking at Rabobank, advising on sustainability at De Labelverbeteraar, and navigating Amsterdam's canals as a licensed skipper.

I believe the most valuable professionals are those who can bridge analytical rigor with human insight — those who see both the numbers and the story behind them. That is who I am working to become.

Professional Experience

Rabobank · Amsterdam

Business Banker &
Financing Advisor

Advised entrepreneurs and SMEs on financing solutions, investment opportunities, and business growth. Assessed financing applications, analyzed financial statements, and conducted credit and risk assessments — gaining deep insight into how capital allocation drives long-term growth.

Master's Coursework

Courses Followed
MSc Business Administration — VU Amsterdam

Financial Management

A solid foundation in corporate finance and valuation. Concepts such as DCF valuation, WACC, capital structure, investment appraisal, and M&A are directly relevant to my interest in real estate investments and asset management.

DCF ValuationWACC Capital StructureInvestment Appraisal Corporate ValuationRisk & ReturnM&A

International Management

How do organizations compete in international markets? This course covered global strategy, competitive advantage, international expansion, and strategic decision-making — valuable insights for navigating complex international investment environments.

Global StrategyInternational Expansion Competitive AdvantageMultinational Organizations Strategic Decision-Making

Alliances, Mergers & Networks

Strategic alliances, mergers, acquisitions, joint ventures, and business networks. This course deepened my interest in investment strategy and corporate growth by showing how collaboration can be a source of competitive advantage.

Strategic PartnershipsMergers & Acquisitions Alliance GovernanceNetwork Theory Value CreationPost-Merger Integration

Global Innovation & Entrepreneurship

How do organizations identify and exploit new opportunities? This course encouraged entrepreneurial thinking and strengthened my ability to recognize and evaluate opportunities — within both startups and large corporations.

Innovation ManagementCorporate Entrepreneurship Business Model InnovationOpportunity Recognition Growth Strategies

International Consulting Project

A practice-based project in which I worked with a multidisciplinary team on a real strategic challenge for an external organization — from research and analysis through to recommendations and presentations to senior stakeholders.

Business AnalysisStakeholder Management Strategic RecommendationsConsulting Teamwork

Culture in International Organizations

How do cultural differences affect organizations and leadership? This course strengthened my understanding of cross-cultural management, diversity, and how culture influences decision-making and organizational performance.

Organizational CultureCross-Cultural Management Leadership Across CulturesInternational Communication Diversity & Inclusion

Research & Thesis

ESG
RE

Strategic Decision-Making in Real Estate Sustainability

"How do real estate managers strategically evaluate sustainability investments under regulatory and market uncertainty?"

My master's thesis investigates how investment professionals in the European real estate sector navigate increasing ESG pressure — from frameworks like the EU Taxonomy, SFDR, and EPBD — while facing uncertainty around future regulation, tenant demand, financing conditions, and asset valuation.

The research is based on qualitative interviews with professionals in asset management, investment management, portfolio management, fund management, and ESG management. It draws on theories of strategic management, institutional theory, sensemaking, and decision-making under uncertainty.

ESG Regulations
Energy Efficiency
Real Estate Investment
Portfolio Management
Regulatory Uncertainty
Capital Allocation
Sustainable Finance
Strategic Decision-Making

Beyond Work

Water, Fitness
& Personal Growth

⚓ Licensed Skipper

Growing up in Friesland — a province deeply connected to water sports and sailing culture — shaped who I am. The water has always been a natural part of my life, teaching me responsibility, independence, and situational awareness from an early age.

In Amsterdam, I regularly work as a skipper on the city's iconic canals, navigating groups through private and corporate events. What makes this unique is that it never feels like work — it combines leadership, hospitality, and a genuine passion for being on the water.

Through my experience as a skipper I have developed leadership under pressure, decision-making in dynamic situations, and communication with diverse groups — skills that translate directly into my professional ambitions.

Klein Vaarbewijs I Klein Vaarbewijs II

🏋️ Fitness & Continuous Development

I strongly believe in continuous self-development, both professionally and personally. Fitness and strength training play an important role in my daily routine — developing discipline, perseverance, and a goal-oriented mindset that carries over into my academic and professional life.

🌐 Building Meaningful Connections

Whether through Young Rabo, Fresh Students, university projects, or industry events, I actively seek opportunities to connect with ambitious and inspiring people. I view networking not as a transactional activity, but as a genuine opportunity to learn, share knowledge, and create lasting relationships.

Outside of work and studies, I follow developments in real estate, finance, investing, entrepreneurship, sustainability, and business strategy — staying constantly curious about the sectors that drive me.
